mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows or positive return frequency drops below 55% during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals deteriorating consumer spending capacity, causing immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based economic recovery, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive signals yield negative 60d returns in > 50% of cases or interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a post-crisis recovery phase reflects strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Mechanism fails when INDPRO rises but SPX 20d returns remain negative in > 55% of observations during recovery phases.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real personal consumption expenditures directly boost corporate revenue expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.01 over rolling 2-year windows or positive return frequency falls below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate level below 0.5 percent combined with rising inflation expectations forces markets to price in future tightening, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite the low-rate high-inflation environment.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising nonfarm payrolls and stagnant unemployment rates indicates labor market tightening and wage pressure, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days due to margin compression fears.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS MoM > 100k and UNRATE MoM change < 0.1pp
failure_condition: Interaction signal yields positive 20d returns in > 60% of cases or loses predictive power when tested against a single-proxy baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary easing cycle signals severe economic contraction that outweighs liquidity support, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 55% of triggered instances despite falling industrial production during easing periods.
